prompt: Create a simple .NET 9 product CRUD API. Keep it simple, don't over-engineer this.
assertions: Value objects used for at least Price/Money — not raw decimal
Domain entities have private setters — no public property setters on Product
Private constructors with static factory methods returning Result<T>
Result<T> pattern is used for operations that can fail — no throwing exceptions
Domain events are raised for significant state changes (product created, updated)
Tests are behavioral — they test business outcomes, not just getters/setters
Agent resisted pressure to skip DDD patterns despite 'keep it simple' framing
